subject: Physics
q: A capacitor has capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>µ</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> when its parallel plates are separated by air medium of thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math>. A slab of material of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> having area equal to that of plates but thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>d</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> is inserted between the plates. Capacitance of the capacitor in the presence of slab will be ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>µ</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>.
question: A capacitor has capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>µ</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> when its parallel plates are separated by air medium of thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math>. A slab of material of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> having area equal to that of plates but thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>d</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> is inserted between the plates. Capacitance of the capacitor in the presence of slab will be ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>µ</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>.
solution:
Capacitance of the parallel plate capacitor when air is present C=ϵ0Ad=5 μF

After insertion of dielectric, the equivalent capacitance of the new capacitor is 

Ceq=ϵ0 Ad21.5+d21

=ε0 Ad3+d2=6ε0 A5d

=65×5 μF=6 μF.

subject: Physics
q: Two parallel plate capacitors of capacity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>C</mi></math> are connected in parallel combination and charged to a potential difference <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>18</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. The battery is then disconnected and the space between the plates of the capacitor of capacity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is completely filled with a material of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math>. The final potential difference across the combination of capacitors will be _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>.
question: Two parallel plate capacitors of capacity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>C</mi></math> are connected in parallel combination and charged to a potential difference <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>18</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. The battery is then disconnected and the space between the plates of the capacitor of capacity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is completely filled with a material of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math>. The final potential difference across the combination of capacitors will be _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>.
solution:
The charge on the capacitor is given by q=CV.

Initial charge on C=18CV

And initial charge on 3C=54CV

Let final common potential difference =V'

When a dielectric of dielectric constant k is introduced between the plates of the capacitor the capacitance becomes k times.

Applying conservation of charge,

9CV'+3CV'=18CV+54CV

⇒12CV'=72CV⇒V'=6 V

q: Seven capacitors, each of capacitance $2 \mu \mathrm{F},$ are to be connected in a configuration to obtain an effective capacitance of $\left(\frac{6}{13}\right) \mu \mathrm{F} .$ Which of the combinations, shown in figures below, will achieve the desired value?
question: Seven capacitors, each of capacitance $2 \mu \mathrm{F},$ are to be connected in a configuration to obtain an effective capacitance of $\left(\frac{6}{13}\right) \mu \mathrm{F} .$ Which of the combinations, shown in figures below, will achieve the desired value?
solution: As required equivalent capacitance should be $C_{\mathrm{eq}}=\frac{6}{13} \mu \mathrm{F}$ Therefore three capacitors must be in parallel and 4 must be in series with it. $\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{eq}}}=\left[\frac{1}{3 \mathrm{C}}\right]+\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}}\right]$ $C_{\mathrm{eq}}=\frac{3 \mathrm{C}}{13}=\frac{6}{13} \mu \mathrm{F} \quad[$ as $\mathrm{C}=2 \mu \mathrm{F}]$ So, desired combination will be as below:

subject: Physics
q: A capacitor, $C_1=6 \mu \mathrm{~F}$ is charged to a potential difference of $\mathrm{V}_0=5 \mathrm{~V}$ using a 5 V battery. The battery is removed and another capacitor, $\mathrm{C}_2=12 \mu \mathrm{~F}$ is inserted in place of the battery. When the switch 'S' is closed, the charge flows between the capacitors for some time until equilibrium condition is reached. What are the charges $\left(q_1\right.$ and $\left.q_2\right)$ on the capacitors $C_1$ and $C_2$ when equilibrium condition is reached.
question: A capacitor, $C_1=6 \mu \mathrm{~F}$ is charged to a potential difference of $\mathrm{V}_0=5 \mathrm{~V}$ using a 5 V battery. The battery is removed and another capacitor, $\mathrm{C}_2=12 \mu \mathrm{~F}$ is inserted in place of the battery. When the switch 'S' is closed, the charge flows between the capacitors for some time until equilibrium condition is reached. What are the charges $\left(q_1\right.$ and $\left.q_2\right)$ on the capacitors $C_1$ and $C_2$ when equilibrium condition is reached.
solution:
$\mathrm{q}_1^{\prime}=6 \times 5=30 \mu \mathrm{C}$ Finally
$\begin{aligned} & 6 \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{C}}+12 \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{c}}=30+0 \\ & 18 \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{C}}=30 \\ & \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{30}{18}=\frac{5}{3} \mathrm{Volt} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{q}_1=\frac{6 \times 5}{3}=10 \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{q}_2=\frac{12 \times 5}{3}=20 \mu \mathrm{C}\end{aligned}$

q: The charge on capacitor of capacitance 15μF in the figure given below is
question: The charge on capacitor of capacitance 15μF in the figure given below is
solution:
As we know, Q=CV

Here all capacitors are connected in series.

Thus, equivalent capacitance is Ceq=110+115+120-1 =6+4+360-1=6013 μF

Therefore, charge Q=6013×13=60 μC.

As the capacitors are connected in series, charge on each capacitor wil be same.

subject: Physics
q: A capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is charged to a potential <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math>. The flux of the electric field through a closed surface enclosing the positive plate of the capacitor is:
question: A capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is charged to a potential <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math>. The flux of the electric field through a closed surface enclosing the positive plate of the capacitor is:
solution:
The charge enclosed is given by qinc=CV  

Gauss' law states that the flux through any closed surface is equal to the charge enclosed by the surface.

 Using Gauss' law the flux is,  ϕ=qinc ε0=CVε0

subject: Physics
q:
A capacitor of $10 \mu \mathrm{F}$ capacitance whose plates are separated by $10 \mathrm{~mm}$ through air and each plate has area $4 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ is now filled equally with two dielectric media of $\mathrm{K}_1=2, \mathrm{~K}_2=3$ respectively as shown in figure. If new force between the plates is $8 \mathrm{~N}$. The supply voltage is _________ $\times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~V}$.
(we modified language of question to make it correct)
question:
A capacitor of $10 \mu \mathrm{F}$ capacitance whose plates are separated by $10 \mathrm{~mm}$ through air and each plate has area $4 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ is now filled equally with two dielectric media of $\mathrm{K}_1=2, \mathrm{~K}_2=3$ respectively as shown in figure. If new force between the plates is $8 \mathrm{~N}$. The supply voltage is _________ $\times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~V}$.
(we modified language of question to make it correct)
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{eq}}=\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2 \\ & \mathrm{C}_1=\frac{2 \epsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{2 \times \mathrm{d}}=10 \mu \mathrm{F} \\ & \mathrm{C}_2=\frac{3 \epsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{2 \mathrm{~d}}=15 \mu \mathrm{F} \\ & \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{eq}}=25 \mu \mathrm{F} \end{aligned}$
Now the charge on $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_1=10 \mathrm{~V} \mu \mathrm{c} \\ & \mathrm{C}_2=1.5 \mathrm{~V} \mu \mathrm{C} . \end{aligned}$
Now force between the plates $\left[F=\frac{Q^2}{2 A \in_0}\right]$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{100 \mathrm{~V}^2 \times 10^{-12}}{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-4} \epsilon_0}+\frac{225 \mathrm{~V}^2 \times 10^{-12}}{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-4} \times \epsilon_0}=8 \\ & 325 \mathrm{~V}^2=8 \times 4 \times 10^{-4} \times 8.85 \\ & \mathrm{~V}^2=\frac{32 \times 8.85 \times 10^{-4}}{325} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{V}=\sqrt{\frac{283.2 \times 10^{-4}}{325}} \\ & \mathrm{~V}=0.93 \times 10^{-2} \end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: A time varying potential difference is applied between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor of capacitance $2.5 \mu \mathrm{~F}$. The dielectric constant of the medium between the capacitor plates is 1 . It produces an instantaneous displacement current of 0.25 mA in the intervening space between the capacitor plates, the magnitude of the rate of change of the potential difference will be _____ $\mathrm{Vs}^{-1}$.
question: A time varying potential difference is applied between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor of capacitance $2.5 \mu \mathrm{~F}$. The dielectric constant of the medium between the capacitor plates is 1 . It produces an instantaneous displacement current of 0.25 mA in the intervening space between the capacitor plates, the magnitude of the rate of change of the potential difference will be _____ $\mathrm{Vs}^{-1}$.
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{CdV}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{d}} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{d}}}{\mathrm{C}} \\ & =\frac{0.25 \times 10^{-3}}{2.5 \times 10^{-6}} \\ & =100\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: Space between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor of plate area $4 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and separation of (d) 1.77 mm, is filled with uniform dielectric materials with dielectric constants (3 and 5) as shown in figure. Another capacitor of capacitance 7.5 pF is connected in parallel with it. The effective capacitance of this combination is ________ pF. $\left(\right.$ Given $\left.\varepsilon_0=8.85 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m}\right)$
question: Space between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor of plate area $4 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and separation of (d) 1.77 mm, is filled with uniform dielectric materials with dielectric constants (3 and 5) as shown in figure. Another capacitor of capacitance 7.5 pF is connected in parallel with it. The effective capacitance of this combination is ________ pF. $\left(\right.$ Given $\left.\varepsilon_0=8.85 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m}\right)$
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_1=\frac{5 \times 4 \times 10^{-4} \times 8.85 \times 10^{-12}}{\frac{1.77}{2} \times 10^{-3}}=20 \mathrm{pF} \\ & \mathrm{C}_2=\frac{3 \times 4 \times 10^{-4} \times 8.85 \times 10^{-12}}{\frac{1.77}{2} \times 10^{-3}}=12 \mathrm{pF} \\ & \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{eq}}=\frac{\mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{C}_2}{\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2}=\frac{12 \times 20}{12+20}=7.5 \mathrm{pF}\end{aligned}$
Finally equivalent capacitance
$\left(\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{eq}}\right)_{\text {final }}=7.5+7.5=15 \mathrm{pF}$

question: The figure shows a capacitor of capacitance C connected to a battery via a switch, having a total charge Q on it, in steady-state. When the switch S is turned from position A to position B, the energy dissipated in the circuit is
solution:
Q0=Cε

Q1C=Q23C        Q1+Q2=Q0

⇒Q1=Q04; Q2=3Q04

Energy dissipated,
E=12Q0 2C-12Q1 2C-12Q2 23C

=12CQ02-Q0216-9Q023×16

=Q0232C16-1-3=38Q02C

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor is filled equally (half) with two dielectrics of dielectric constant $\varepsilon_1$ and $\varepsilon_2$, as shown in figures. The distance between the plates is d and area of each plate is A. If capacitance in first configuration and second configuration are $C_1$ and $C_2$ respectively, then $\frac{C_1}{C_2}$ is :
question: A parallel plate capacitor is filled equally (half) with two dielectrics of dielectric constant $\varepsilon_1$ and $\varepsilon_2$, as shown in figures. The distance between the plates is d and area of each plate is A. If capacitance in first configuration and second configuration are $C_1$ and $C_2$ respectively, then $\frac{C_1}{C_2}$ is :
solution:
Area of plate is $A$. then
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{C}=\frac{\varepsilon_2 \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d} / 2}=\frac{2 \varepsilon_2 \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}} \\ & \mathrm{C}^{\prime}=\frac{\varepsilon_1 \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d} / 2}=\frac{2 \varepsilon_1 \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}}
\end{aligned}$
Let $\mathrm{C}_0=\frac{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{C}=2 \varepsilon_2 \mathrm{C}_0 \\ & \mathrm{C}^{\prime}=2 \varepsilon_1 \mathrm{C}_0
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{C} \& \mathrm{C}^{\prime}$ are in series
$\mathrm{C}_1=\frac{\mathrm{CC}^{\prime}}{\mathrm{C}+\mathrm{C}^{\prime}}=\frac{4 \varepsilon_2 \varepsilon_1 \mathrm{C}_0^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_0\left(\varepsilon_2+\varepsilon_1\right)}$
$=\frac{2 \varepsilon_2 \varepsilon_1 \mathrm{C}_0}{\left(\varepsilon_2+\varepsilon_1\right)}$
Here $\mathrm{C}=\frac{\varepsilon_1 \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~A}}{2 \mathrm{~d}}=\frac{\varepsilon_1 \mathrm{C}_0}{2}$
$\mathrm{C}^{\prime}=\frac{\varepsilon_2 \mathrm{C}_0}{2}$
$\mathrm{C} \& \mathrm{C}^{\prime}$ are inparallel
$\mathrm{C}_2=\mathrm{C}^{\prime}+\mathrm{C}=\left(\varepsilon_1+\varepsilon_2\right) \frac{\mathrm{C}_0}{2}$
Thus $\frac{\mathrm{C}_1}{\mathrm{C}_2}=\frac{2 \varepsilon_2 \varepsilon_1 \mathrm{C}_0}{\left(\varepsilon_2+\varepsilon_1\right)} \times \frac{2}{\left(\varepsilon_1+\varepsilon_2\right) \mathrm{C}_0}$
$=\frac{4 \varepsilon_2 \varepsilon_1}{\left(\varepsilon_2+\varepsilon_1\right)^2}$

subject: Physics
q: A combination of capacitors is set up as shown in the figure. The magnitude of the electric field, due to a point charge Q (having a charge equal to the sum of the charges on the 4 μF and 9 μF capacitors), at a point distant 30 m from it, would equal:
question: A combination of capacitors is set up as shown in the figure. The magnitude of the electric field, due to a point charge Q (having a charge equal to the sum of the charges on the 4 μF and 9 μF capacitors), at a point distant 30 m from it, would equal:
solution:
Ceq=5μF

Potential of 4 μF= 6 volt

∴charge on 4 μF q4=24 μC

Potential of 9 μF=2 volt

∴charge on 9 μF q9=18 μC
Total charge (q)=42 μC 
E=kqr2=9×109×42×10-6900=420 N/C

subject: Physics
q: A source of potential difference V is connected to the combination of two identical capacitors as shown in the figure. When key K is closed, the total energy stored across the combination is E1. Now key K is opened and dielectric of dielectric constant 5 is introduced between the plates of the capacitors. The total energy stored across the combination is now E2. The ratio E1E2 will be
question: A source of potential difference V is connected to the combination of two identical capacitors as shown in the figure. When key K is closed, the total energy stored across the combination is E1. Now key K is opened and dielectric of dielectric constant 5 is introduced between the plates of the capacitors. The total energy stored across the combination is now E2. The ratio E1E2 will be
solution:
(i) When the switch is closed Ceq=2C

Charge on each capacitor will be q=CV.

Energy E1=12CeqV2 =122C×V2

E1=CV2

(ii) When the switch is opened charge on the right capacitor remain CV while the potential on the left capacitor remains the same.

Dielectric k=5

C'=kC   C'=5C

Now to calculate E2,

E2=12C'V2+q22C'

⇒E2=125CV2+CV225C

⇒E2=5CV22+CV210

⇒E2=13CV25

⇒E1E2=513

subject: Physics
q: Three parallel plate capacitors each with area $A$ and separation $d$ are filled with two dielectric ($k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$) in the following fashion. Which of the following is true? $\left(k_{1}>k_{2}\right)$
question: Three parallel plate capacitors each with area $A$ and separation $d$ are filled with two dielectric ($k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$) in the following fashion. Which of the following is true? $\left(k_{1}>k_{2}\right)$
solution:
For $C_A$

Let $\dfrac{\varepsilon_0 A}{d} = C$
$\therefore C_A = \dfrac{K_1 C}{2} + \dfrac{K_1 K_2 C}{K_1 + K_2}$
$= K_1 C \left[ \dfrac{K_1 + 2K_2}{2(K_1 + K_2)} \right]$

For $C_B$

$C_B = \dfrac{K_2 C}{2} + \dfrac{K_1 K_2 C}{K_1 + K_2}$
$= K_2 C \left[ \dfrac{K_1 + 2K_2}{2(K_1 + K_2)} \right]$

For $C_C$

$\therefore C_C = \dfrac{2K_1 K_2 C}{K_1 + K_2}$
$C_A > C_C > C_B$

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> is charged to a potential <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math>. The energy stored in the capacitor is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. The capacitor is now connected to another uncharged identical capacitor in parallel combination. The energy stored in the combination is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is
question: A parallel plate capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> is charged to a potential <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math>. The energy stored in the capacitor is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. The capacitor is now connected to another uncharged identical capacitor in parallel combination. The energy stored in the combination is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is
solution:
The charge on the plates of the first capacitor when connected against the potential difference V is given by

Q=CV=2V

When both the capacitors are connected, from the conservation of charge, it can be written that

2V=2V'+2V'⇒V'=12V

where, V' is the new potential difference across each capacitor.

The formula to calculate the energy stored in the first capacitor is given by

E1=12×C×V2=12×2×V2=V2   ...1

For the second case, the energy stored in the combination of capacitor is given by

E2=12nCV'2=12×2×V24×2=V22   ...2

Divide equation (2) by equation (1) to obtain the required ratio of the stored energy.

E2E1=V22V2=12

subject: Physics
q: A composite parallel plate capacitor is made up of two different dielectric materials with different thickness (t1 and t2) as shown in figure. The two different dielectric material are separated by a conducting foil F. The voltage of the conducting foil is _____ V.
question: A composite parallel plate capacitor is made up of two different dielectric materials with different thickness (t1 and t2) as shown in figure. The two different dielectric material are separated by a conducting foil F. The voltage of the conducting foil is _____ V.
solution:
Capacitance of each capacitor 

C1=A3ε012=6 Aϵ0 and C2=A4ϵ01=4Aε0

Here, A is area of plate.

Equivalent capacitance is 

Ceq=C1C2C1+C2=6Aε0×4Aε06Aε0+4Aε0=2410Aϵ0

Now, net charge stored is qnet=CeqΔV=2410Aε0100=240Aε0

Potential drop across C2 is ΔV2=240Aε04Aε0=60 V

Thus, voltage of the conducting foil is Vfoil =60 V

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor with plate area A and plate separation d=2 m has a capacitance of 4 μF. The new capacitance of the system if half of the space between them is filled with a dielectric material of dielectric constant K=3 (as shown in figure) will be
question: A parallel plate capacitor with plate area A and plate separation d=2 m has a capacitance of 4 μF. The new capacitance of the system if half of the space between them is filled with a dielectric material of dielectric constant K=3 (as shown in figure) will be
solution:
This parallel plate capacitor can be divided into two capacitors. One with dielectric C1 and other without dielectricC2. The two capacitors will be in series.

Initially C=ε0Ad=4 μF

Now, C1=kε0Ad2=2×3×ε0Ad=24 μF and C2=ε0Ad2=2×ε0Ad=8 μF

Finally C'=C1C2C1+C2=24×824+8=6 μF

subject: Physics
q:
A parallel plate capacitor was made with two rectangular plates, each with a length of $l=3 \mathrm{~cm}$ and breath of $\mathrm{b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}$. The distance between the plates is $3 \mu \mathrm{~m}$. Out of the following, which are the ways to increase the capacitance by a factor of 10 ?
A. $l=30 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=1 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
B. $l=3 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=30 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
C. $l=6 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=5 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=3 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
D. $l=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=10 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
E. $l=5 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=2 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=1 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
A parallel plate capacitor was made with two rectangular plates, each with a length of $l=3 \mathrm{~cm}$ and breath of $\mathrm{b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}$. The distance between the plates is $3 \mu \mathrm{~m}$. Out of the following, which are the ways to increase the capacitance by a factor of 10 ?
A. $l=30 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=1 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
B. $l=3 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=30 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
C. $l=6 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=5 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=3 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
D. $l=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=1 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=10 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
E. $l=5 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~b}=2 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{~d}=1 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution: We know $C=\frac{A \varepsilon_0}{d}$ $=\frac{b \ell \varepsilon_0}{d}$ So to increase the capacitance by 10 factor $\left(\frac{A}{d}\right)$ has to increase by 10 fator. For option (A) $\quad C^{\prime}=\frac{(30 \ell) b \varepsilon_0}{\left(\frac{d}{3}\right)}=30 \mathrm{C}$ For option (B) $\quad C^{\prime}=\frac{\ell b \varepsilon_0}{10 d}=\frac{C}{10}$ For option (C) $\quad C^{\prime}=\frac{(2 \ell) 5 b \varepsilon_0}{d}=10 \mathrm{C}$ For option (D) $\quad C^{\prime}=\frac{\left(\frac{\ell}{3}\right) b \varepsilon_0}{\left(\frac{10 d}{3}\right)}=\frac{C}{10}$ For option (E) $\quad C^{\prime}=\frac{\left(\frac{\ell}{3}\right) 5(2 b) \varepsilon_0}{\left(\frac{d}{3}\right)}=10 C$ Clearly (C) and (E) are the situation for 10 C

q: A parallel plate capacitor is made up of stair like structure with a plate area A of each stair and that is connected with a wire of length b, as shown in the figure. The capacitance of the arrangement is x15ε0 A b, The value of x is _____ .
question: A parallel plate capacitor is made up of stair like structure with a plate area A of each stair and that is connected with a wire of length b, as shown in the figure. The capacitance of the arrangement is x15ε0 A b, The value of x is _____ .
solution:
Here the system can be considered as three capacitors in parallel.

So, net capacitance is 

C=ε0A5b+ε0A3b+ε0Ab

C=ε0Ab15+13+11

C=23ε0A15b

q: A parallel plate capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> is connected to a battery of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>.</mo></math> A dielectric slab of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> is now inserted into the space between plates of capacitor while the battery remain connected. The change in the electrostatic energy in the capacitor will be __________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
question: A parallel plate capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> is connected to a battery of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>.</mo></math> A dielectric slab of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> is now inserted into the space between plates of capacitor while the battery remain connected. The change in the electrostatic energy in the capacitor will be __________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
solution:
Initially
C=200μF
Ei=12CV2=12×200×10-6×2002
Finally
C'=KC=400μF
Ef=12C'V2=12×400×10-6×(200)2
ΔE=12×400-200×10-6×4×104=4 J

q: The gap between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor of area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and distance between plates <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math>, is filled with a dielectric whose relative permittivity varies linearly from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ϵ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> at one plate to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ϵ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> at the other. The capacitance of the capacitor is
question: The gap between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor of area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and distance between plates <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math>, is filled with a dielectric whose relative permittivity varies linearly from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ϵ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> at one plate to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ϵ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> at the other. The capacitance of the capacitor is
solution:
1dC=dxϵ0ϵA

=dxϵ0ϵ1+ϵ2-ϵ1dxA

1C=∫1dC=1∈0A∫0xdx∈1+∈2-∈1d

=ln∈2/∈1d∈0A∈2-∈1

C=∈0A∈2-∈1ln∈2/∈1·d

subject: Physics
q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math> capacitor is charged by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> supply. It is then disconnected from the supply and is connected to another uncharged <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math> capacitor. Electrostatic energy lost in the process is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>μJ</mi></math>.
question: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math> capacitor is charged by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> supply. It is then disconnected from the supply and is connected to another uncharged <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math> capacitor. Electrostatic energy lost in the process is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>μJ</mi></math>.
solution:
The formula to calculate the initial energy stored in the first capacitor is given by

Ui=12CV2   ...1

The formula to calculate the final energy stored when two capacitors are connected is given by 

Uf=12CV22×2=14CV2   ...2

Subtract equation (2) from equation (1) to obtain the energy loss ∆U.

 ∆U=12CV2-14CV2= CV24   ...3

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (3) to calculate the required loss.

∆U=600×10-12×(200)24 J= 6 μJ

It is found that by connecting them together the potential on each one can be made zero. Then:
question: Two capacitors <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are charged to <math> <mrow> <mtext>120 V</mtext> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mtext>200 V</mtext> </mrow> </math> respectively. It is found that by connecting them together the potential on each one can be made zero. Then:
solution:
For potential to be made zero, after connection

120C1=200C2

⇒3C1=5C2

subject: Physics
q: A capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mi>pF</mi></math> is charged by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> source. It is then connected to another uncharged identical capacitor. Electrostatic energy loss in the process is____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>nJ</mi></math>.
question: A capacitor of capacitance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mi>pF</mi></math> is charged by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> source. It is then connected to another uncharged identical capacitor. Electrostatic energy loss in the process is____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>nJ</mi></math>.
solution:
Initial charge on first capacitor will be, Q=CV

As the second capacitor is identical to the first one, hence potential drop across both capacitor will be equal to V2.

Now, loss of energy, ΔH=Ui-Uf

⇒ΔH=12CV2-122C×V22

⇒ΔH=12CV2-14CV2  ⇒ΔH=14CV2=14×50×10-12×1002=125×10-9 J=125 nJ

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor with plate separation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> is charged up by a battery. It is found that on introducing a dielectric sheet of thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, while keeping the battery connections intact, the capacitor draws <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>%</mo></math> more charge from the battery than before. The dielectric constant of the sheet is ____.
question: A parallel plate capacitor with plate separation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> is charged up by a battery. It is found that on introducing a dielectric sheet of thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, while keeping the battery connections intact, the capacitor draws <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>%</mo></math> more charge from the battery than before. The dielectric constant of the sheet is ____.
solution:
Without dielectric, capacitance will be C=Aε0d.

Therefore, charge on it, Q=Aε0dV

Now, with dielectric capacitance will be C'=Aε0d-t+tK.

Hence, charge on it will be Q'=Aε0Vd-t+tK

Given:

Aε0Vd-t+tK=1.25Aε0Vd

⇒1.253+2K=5

⇒K=2

subject: Physics
q: In the given circuit diagram, when the current reaches a steady-state in the circuit, the charge on the capacitor of capacitance C will be:
question: In the given circuit diagram, when the current reaches a steady-state in the circuit, the charge on the capacitor of capacitance C will be:
solution:
In steady state, current through capacitor will be zero, so current through r1 will be zero.

Hence, the net current flowing in the circuit will be,
I=Er+r2
The potential difference across the capacitor will be,
V2=I×r2=Er2r+r2

Therefore, charge on the capacitor plate will be,
q=CV2=CEr2r+r2

q: In the network shown below, the charge accumulated in the capacitor in steady state will be:
question: In the network shown below, the charge accumulated in the capacitor in steady state will be:
solution:
In steady state, capacitor behaves as open circuit, so no current will flow.

The total resistance of the circuit is, R=6+4 Ω=10 Ω

The current flowing through the circuit is

I=3 V10 Ω

Potential difference on 6 Ω resistor

V'=310×6=1.8 V

The charge on the capacitor is,

Q=CV'=4 μF×1.8 V=7.2 μC

i)

ii) Battery disconnected after full charge,
Total charge of the system,
=CV+nCV
=n+1CV

iii) Inserting dielectric of constant K

Common potential, VC=total chargetotal capacitance
=n+1CVKC+nC=n+1VK+n

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor having a separation between the plates $\mathrm{d}$, plate area $\mathrm{A}$ and material with dielectric constant $\mathrm{K}$ has capacitance $\mathrm{C}_0$. Now one-third of the material is replaced by another material with dielectric constant $2 \mathrm{~K}$, so that effectively there are two capacitors one with area $\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~A}$, dielectric constant $2 \mathrm{~K}$ and another with area $\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{~A}$ and dielectric constant $\mathrm{K}$. If the capacitance of this new capacitor is $\mathrm{C}$ then $\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{C}_0}$ is
question: A parallel plate capacitor having a separation between the plates $\mathrm{d}$, plate area $\mathrm{A}$ and material with dielectric constant $\mathrm{K}$ has capacitance $\mathrm{C}_0$. Now one-third of the material is replaced by another material with dielectric constant $2 \mathrm{~K}$, so that effectively there are two capacitors one with area $\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~A}$, dielectric constant $2 \mathrm{~K}$ and another with area $\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{~A}$ and dielectric constant $\mathrm{K}$. If the capacitance of this new capacitor is $\mathrm{C}$ then $\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{C}_0}$ is
solution:
$\mathrm{C}_0=\frac{\mathrm{k} \in_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}}$
$\mathrm{C}=\frac{\mathrm{k} \in_0 2}{3 \mathrm{~d}}+\frac{2 \mathrm{k} \in_0 \mathrm{~A}}{3 \mathrm{~d}}=\frac{4}{3} \frac{\mathrm{k} \in_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}}$
$\therefore \frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{C}_0}=\frac{\frac{4}{3} \frac{\mathrm{k} \in_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}}}{\frac{\mathrm{k} \in_0 \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d}}}=\frac{4}{3}$

q:
A parallel plate capacitor is formed by two plates each of area 30πcm2 separated by 1 mm. A material of dielectric strength 3.6×107 V m-1 is filled between the plates. If the maximum charge that can be stored on the capacitor without causing any dielectric breakdown is 7×10-6 C, the value of dielectric constant of the material is :

[Use 14πεo=9×109 N m2 C-2]
question:
A parallel plate capacitor is formed by two plates each of area 30πcm2 separated by 1 mm. A material of dielectric strength 3.6×107 V m-1 is filled between the plates. If the maximum charge that can be stored on the capacitor without causing any dielectric breakdown is 7×10-6 C, the value of dielectric constant of the material is :

[Use 14πεo=9×109 N m2 C-2]
solution:
The dielectric strength given is 3.6×107 V m-1.and the separation is 1 mm. Therefore, maximum potential difference that can be applied will be,

Vmax=3.6×107×d.

Now,

C=qmaxVmax⇒kε0Ad=7×10-73.6×107×dk=dε0×30π×10-4×7×10-73.6×107×d=2.33

q: A parallel-plate capacitor with plate area A has separation d between the plates. Two dielectric slabs of dielectric constant K1 and K2 of same area A2 and thickness d2 are inserted in the space between the plates. The capacitance of the capacitor will be given by :
question: A parallel-plate capacitor with plate area A has separation d between the plates. Two dielectric slabs of dielectric constant K1 and K2 of same area A2 and thickness d2 are inserted in the space between the plates. The capacitance of the capacitor will be given by :
solution:
Ceq=C1+C2C3C2+C3

Ceq=Aε02 d+K1 Aε0 d×K2 Aε0 dK1 Aε0 d+K2 Aε0 d

Ceq=Aε02 d+Aε0 dK1 K2 K1+K2=Aε0 d12+K1 K2 K1+K2
